2604.05899.title: FRENCH-YMCA: A FRENCH Corpus meeting the language needs of Youth, froM Children to Adolescents
2604.05899.paper_summary: In this paper, we introduce the French-YMCA corpus, a new linguistic resource specifically tailored for children and adolescents. The motivation for building this corpus is clear: children have unique language requirements, as their language skills are in constant evolution and differ from those of adults. With an extensive collection of 39,200 text files, the French-YMCA corpus encompasses a total of 22,471,898 words. It distinguishes itself through its diverse sources, consistent grammar and spelling, and the commitment to providing open online accessibility for all. Such corpus can serve as the foundation for training language models that understand and anticipate youth's language, thereby enhancing the quality of digital interactions and ensuring that responses and suggestions are age-appropriate and adapted to the comprehension level of users of this age.
2604.05899.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們介紹了法國YMCA語料庫，這是一個專門為兒童和青少年量身打造的新語言資源。建立這個語料庫的動機非常明確：兒童有獨特的語言需求，因為他們的語言技能不斷發展，並且與成人的語言能力不同。法國YMCA語料庫擁有39,200個文本文件，總計22,471,898個單詞。它通過多樣的來源、一致的語法和拼寫，以及致力於提供所有人都能在線訪問的開放性，來區別於其他語料庫。這樣的語料庫可以作為訓練語言模型的基礎，這些模型能夠理解和預測年輕人的語言，從而提高數字互動的質量，確保回應和建議適合年齡並適應這一年齡段用戶的理解水平。
2604.05899.author: Cherifa Ben Khelil et.al.
2604.05899.authors: Cherifa Ben Khelil, Jean-Yves Antoine, Anaïs Halftermeyer, Frédéric Rayar, Mathieu Thebaud
